projectDescription: A modern continuation of DOSBox that emulates the MS-DOS operating environment on current hardware, with advanced features, improved audio/video accuracy, and cross-platform support.
keyFeatures: MS-DOS and Windows 3.1 emulation on modern hardware (Windows, macOS, Linux)
High-fidelity audio emulation: AdLib, Sound Blaster, Roland MT-32, General MIDI, Gravis UltraSound
Accurate graphics emulation: Hercules, CGA, EGA, VGA, SVGA, 3dfx Voodoo
Flexible layered configuration system (global + per-game configs)
HTTP REST API for external tool integration and memory inspection
Modern build system (CMake + vcpkg) with C++20 codebase
Cross-platform: Windows, macOS, Linux (x86_64 and ARM)
Drop-in replacement for legacy DOSBox with backward-compatible configurations
Advanced shader and rendering pipeline with aspect-ratio correction
Networking support: IPX, serial ports, TCP/IP via libslirp
publicApiSurface: dosbox [PATH] — launch emulator with a game directory, disk image, or executable
--set <setting>=<value> — override a configuration setting at launch
--printconf — print the path to the primary config file
--editconf — open primary config in the default text editor
GET /api/cpu — read x86 CPU registers via HTTP API
GET /api/memory/:offset/:len — read emulated memory via HTTP API
GET /api/dos/files — list DOS filesystem via HTTP API
[sdl] section — window, fullscreen, and input settings
[cpu] section — CPU type, cycles, and core settings
[render] section — graphics scaling, shaders, and aspect ratio
[mixer] section — audio volume and sample rate settings
[midi] section — MIDI device and soundfont configuration
